description_long: This object is a rectangular manhole cover constructed from a grid of intersecting metal bars, forming a lattice pattern with evenly spaced square openings. The surface is flat and robust, designed to withstand significant weight and provide drainage or ventilation. The edges are straight and clean, with no visible hinges, handles, or locking mechanisms. The color is a dark metallic gray, typical of galvanized or coated steel. The grid structure allows water and debris to pass through while maintaining structural integrity. There are no visible inscriptions, logos, or fasteners on the surface.
description: A flat, rectangular metal manhole cover with a grid lattice pattern and dark metallic finish, designed for drainage and strength.
description_view_0: Front view shows the grid pattern extending across the flat surface, with the thickness of the cover visible at the bottom edge.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the rectangular shape and the open grid structure, revealing the depth and spacing of the bars.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the same grid pattern and thickness, with no additional features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again emphasizes the open lattice design and the uniformity of the grid openings.
attribute_pattern_design: Uniform square grid lattice with intersecting metal bars, no additional motifs or symbols.
